title: Multiple clocks and usage of a generate block results in multi-driven warning
body:
---


Author Name: **Benjamin Geib**
Original Redmine Issue: 979 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-10-19
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

Hey Wilson,

I tried using multiple clocks and a generate block to assign bit values to an output (or register). When linting this using verilator I get the following warnings:
verilator_bin --lint-only example.v
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: example.v:10: Signal has multiple driving blocks: outputs
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: example.v:24: ... Location of first driving block
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: example.v:24: ... Location of other driving block
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: Use "/* verilator lint_off MULTIDRIVEN */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)

This however should be legal Verilog code. Any chance you could fix this in a future release?

Thanks a lot and with best regards
Benjamin
